description: M4 Abilities + ReactFlow — Ability system (skills/plugins/MCPs), agent chain editor, Claude Skill import

userStories.title: Create Abilities CRUD REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to manage Abilities (skills, plugins, MCP servers).
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/abilities.ts as a Fastify plugin
GET /api/abilities returns all abilities for the default workspace
POST /api/abilities creates a new ability with name, type (skill|plugin|mcp), config (JSON)
PUT /api/abilities/:id updates an ability
DELETE /api/abilities/:id removes an ability
GET /api/abilities/:id returns a single ability with full config
Input validation on all POST/PUT bodies
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add ability assignment to agents/stages
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to assign abilities to pipeline stages so agents only access assigned tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/db/schema/stage-abilities.ts junction table with stageId (FK) and abilityId (FK)
Update barrel export in schema/index.ts
Migration generates and runs successfully
Add GET /api/pipelines/:pipelineId/stages/:stageId/abilities endpoint
Add POST /api/pipelines/:pipelineId/stages/:stageId/abilities/:abilityId to assign
Add DELETE /api/pipelines/:pipelineId/stages/:stageId/abilities/:abilityId to unassign
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Claude SKILL.md import parser
userStories.description: As a user, I want to import Claude Code SKILL.md files as Abilities (PRD US-2).
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/abilities/skill-parser.ts with parseSkillMd(content: string) function
Parses SKILL.md frontmatter (name, description, trigger) and body content
Returns { name, description, type: 'skill', config: { trigger, content } }
Add POST /api/abilities/import-skill endpoint accepting raw markdown body
Endpoint parses the SKILL.md and creates an Ability record
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Abilities API hooks for frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TanStack Query hooks to manage abilities from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/abilities.ts with useAbilities(), useAbility(id), useCreateAbility(), useUpdateAbility(), useDeleteAbility() hooks
Add useImportSkill() hook for SKILL.md import
All mutations invalidate the 'abilities' query key
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update Abilities sidebar panel with real API data
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Abilities sidebar to show real abilities from the database (PRD US-3).
acceptanceCriteria: Update LeftSidebar abilities panel to use useAbilities() hook instead of hardcoded ABILITIES array
Remove hardcoded ABILITIES array
Show loading and error states
Clicking an ability navigates to ability-detail view with the ability's data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update AbilityDetailView with real data and edit capability
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and edit ability details in the main view.
acceptanceCriteria: Update AbilityDetailView.tsx to accept an abilityId prop or read from route/store state
Fetch ability data via useAbility(id) hook
Display all fields: name, type, version, active status, config, assigned agents
Add toggle for active/inactive status
Add delete button with confirmation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Install ReactFlow and create base canvas component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ReactFlow canvas for the agent chain editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @xyflow/react as dependency
Create src/components/graph/ChainEditor.tsx with ReactFlow canvas
Dark theme canvas background (#0a0d13) with grid dot pattern
Pan (drag background) and zoom (scroll) enabled
Empty state shows 'Add your first agent node' prompt
Replace GraphView placeholder with ChainEditor
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create custom AgentNode component for ReactFlow
userStories.description: As a user, I want styled agent nodes on the canvas with type dot, name, model, and status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/graph/AgentNode.tsx as a custom ReactFlow node
Node displays: colored type dot, node name (bold 11px), model name (muted 10px)
Shows first 72 chars of system prompt as body preview
Status badge in footer: pending/running/done/error
Open button and delete (x) button
Input port (left) and output port (right) as circular handles
Node card styled with #111827 background and #1e2a3a border
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Node types: Trigger(cyan), Prototyper(violet), Builder(indigo), Tester(emerald), Reviewer(amber), Auditor(red)

userStories.title: Add edge types and connection handling
userStories.description: As a user, I want to connect agent nodes with typed edges (always, on-success, on-failure, on-decision).
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/graph/CustomEdge.tsx as a custom ReactFlow edge
Edge types with colors: always(gray #374151), on-success(emerald), on-failure(red), on-decision(amber)
Smooth bezier curves between output and input ports
Hover reveals delete button at edge midpoint
Cycle detection: reject connections that would create loops
Dashed preview curve during drag connection
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create Add Node menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add new agent nodes to the canvas by type.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/graph/AddNodeMenu.tsx
Menu shows 6 built-in node types: Trigger, Prototyper, Builder, Tester, Reviewer, Auditor
Each type shows its colored dot and default role description
Clicking a type adds a new node at the cursor/center position
Accessible via toolbar '+ Add node' button and double-click on empty canvas
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create node inspector panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a slide-in panel to edit node name, model, system prompt, and abilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/graph/NodeInspector.tsx as a 340px slide-in panel
Opens when clicking 'Open' on a node, slides in from the right
Editable fields: node name, model dropdown (using ModelSelector), system prompt textarea
Abilities section: tag list of assigned abilities with remove button, '+ Add ability' button
Edge condition dropdown: Always/On success/On failure/On decision
Save and Delete buttons
Panel does not dismiss the canvas — nodes remain visible
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add chain editor toolbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toolbar above the canvas with layout, export, and template actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/graph/ChainToolbar.tsx above the canvas
Buttons: + Add node, Auto-layout, Clear edges, Export JSON
Auto-layout arranges nodes in a left-to-right grid
Clear edges removes all edges leaving nodes in place
Export JSON downloads chain definition as .insomniac-chain.json file
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add chain definition persistence to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to save and load chain definitions from the project's database record.
acceptanceCriteria: Add chainDefinition (JSON text) column to projects table via migration
PUT /api/projects/:id/chain saves the chain JSON
GET /api/projects/:id/chain returns the chain JSON
ChainEditor loads chain from API on mount and auto-saves on changes (debounced 1s)
Chain schema matches PRD: { version, nodes[], edges[] }
Typecheck passes
